title: Pack A2 - ADHD follow-through scenario catalog

notes: This file is the progress ledger for pack A2 only. Add one entry per authored scenario, across both surfaces (lifeops-bench Python ids and scenario-runner .scenario.ts ids).
status flips planned -> authored once the scenario file/literal exists; -> verified once a real-LLM run has been captured and hand-reviewed per PR_EVIDENCE.md.

pr-deterministic scenario-runner rows are verified via the keyless strict LLM proxy (no live key needed); live-only rows stay authored until a real-LLM trajectory is captured at the key boundary (#12781).

2026-07-05 verification pass (#14350): the 3 authored scenario-runner rows were re-run LIVE (Cerebras gpt-oss-120b, TZ=UTC, develop@afc2626) and hand-read; all three REPRODUCED a genuine judge-0.00 failure and stay authored (see each row note). Root cause is shared: on read/list-then-summarize turns the LifeOps action-planner emits tool calls but no synthesized user-facing reply reaches Casey, so the non-shaming acknowledge+shrink+consent / wins-first-recap outcome is never produced. Not an authoring defect and not weakened — it needs a runtime fix to the tool-then-reply synthesis path, tracked separately. The 5 already-verified scenario-runner rows and the 16 surface:lifeops-bench rows were not part of this pass.

scenarios.notes: Live provider run passed on 2026-07-04 (runId 7f5a0e94-9427-4ced-b994-9032f3d83382): broad delete request was blocked with noop=true and blockedReason=broad_destructive_delete; result text refused to delete everything and offered pause/snooze or naming one specific item; judge score 1.00; native export strict privacy attestation passed with 0 residual findings.

pr-deterministic (keyless). REAL scheduler tick: a reminder with a user_acknowledged completionCheck fires (once_due), goes unanswered -> no_reply_retry_1 (snooze override, scheduledTaskCompletionTimeouts), re-fires (scheduled_override_due), then settles terminally (skipped/no_reply_reminder_expired). Proves the dropped task is neither lost nor nagged forever. Passes keyless under TZ=UTC SCENARIO_USE_LLM_PROXY=1 SCENARIO_LLM_PROXY_STRICT=1 --conditions eliza-source.

pr-deterministic (keyless). Owner reminderIntensity=persistent (set via the REAL OwnerFactStore) reshapes the SAME no-reply ladder to two retry rungs: no_reply_retry_1 -> re-fire -> no_reply_retry_2 -> re-fire -> terminal skip. Contrasts with the normal (one rung) and minimal (zero rungs) siblings to pin the intensity axis. Passes keyless.

pr-deterministic (keyless). Owner reminderIntensity=minimal drops the retry rung entirely: the fired reminder's FIRST completion timeout is terminal (skipped/no_reply_reminder_expired) with no no_reply_retry_1, and nothing re-arms afterward. The anti-nag low end of the intensity axis. Passes keyless.

pr-deterministic (keyless). Engagement retires the ladder: a fired reminder is acknowledged (fired -> acknowledged via the REST acknowledge verb), so the tick past the followup window that WOULD have produced no_reply_retry_1 produces no timeout and no re-fire. The non-shaming other half of the escalation story. Passes keyless.

Live-only. Missed reminder -> re-plan smaller, no shame/streak language, ask before scheduling (judgeRubric grades tone + shrink-and-consent). Ports lifeops-bench live.adhd.follow.missed_step_replan_without_guilt. Live-verify deferred to the key boundary (#12781). Live-verify attempts 2026-07-04 (Cerebras gpt-oss-120b, TZ=UTC) FAILED — row stays authored: attempt 1's action asked a good clarifier but the runtime's apology fallback swallowed the response; attempt 2 graded genuinely — the model interrogated for date/time/recurrence and re-proposed the full task instead of a neutral, guilt-free smaller-step replan (judge 0.00). Re-verified 2026-07-05 (#14350; Live Cerebras gpt-oss-120b, TZ=UTC, develop@afc2626, runId a362f5dd-e38d-4f91-bb8a-de64d8c60944) — REPRODUCED (judge 0.00): on this requiresTool turn the ACTION_PLANNER emitted a single SCHEDULED_TASKS_LIST tool call with empty text and finishReason=tool-calls, and the turn ended with no synthesized user-facing reply, so Casey received no neutral acknowledgement + one-smaller-step offer + consent — only a serialized tool-call literal. Genuine runtime gap (list/read-then-reply turns don't synthesize a final user-facing message), not an over-narrow assertion; scenario left unweakened.

Live-only. Repeated misses (incl. one mid-conversation) -> offer a smaller habit or a pause, never streak shame, no unprompted identical re-add (judgeRubric). Ports lifeops-bench live.adhd.follow.repeated_miss_shrink_or_pause. Live-verify deferred (#12781). Live-verify attempts 2026-07-04 (Cerebras gpt-oss-120b, TZ=UTC) FAILED — row stays authored: attempt 1 drafted an identical unprompted reminder with no shrink offer; attempt 2 offered no smaller habit or pause, attempted an identical reminder, and misclaimed an app-settings limitation (judge 0.00). Re-verified 2026-07-05 (#14350; Live Cerebras gpt-oss-120b, TZ=UTC, develop@afc2626) — REPRODUCED (judge 0.00): turn 1 routed to OWNER_REMINDERS review (Stage-1 planned a non-lecturing reply) but the turn ended with no user-facing reply surfaced; turn 2 did reply ('I'll stay out of productivity-coach mode…') yet offered neither a smaller stretch nor a pause, so the shrink-or-pause criterion genuinely failed. Behavior-bar gap, not an assertion problem; scenario left unweakened.

Live-only. End-of-day recap leads with wins, neutral carryover framing, consent before creating tomorrow's reminder (judgeRubric grades ordering + framing + consent). Ports lifeops-bench live.adhd.follow.end_of_day_wins_first_recap. Live-verify deferred (#12781). Live-verify attempts 2026-07-04 (Cerebras gpt-oss-120b, TZ=UTC) FAILED — row stays authored: the scenario now seeds real day-state through the definitions + occurrence-completion routes (fixed in this PR — the rubric was previously ungradeable with no stored data), yet the model still answered that it cannot see today's activities and produced no recap (judge 0.00, same shape both graded attempts). Re-verified 2026-07-05 (#14350; Live Cerebras gpt-oss-120b, TZ=UTC, develop@afc2626): the three definition seeds + two occurrence-completion routes all returned 200 (real day-state present — two wins + one open carryover), but the recap turn still produced no recap (judge 0.00) — planner iter1 called SCHEDULED_TASKS_HISTORY which errored MISSING_TASK_ID, the evaluation model then emitted non-JSON (parseError), and iter2's 'recovered user-facing prose' was itself a malformed tool-call literal (call:OWNER_TODOS_REVIEW{action:review}) rather than a wins-first recap. Genuine model+loop gap on read-then-summarize turns; scenario left unweakened.
